cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking which statement about lightning is true according to the passage. The options are A, B, C, D. I need to go through the passage again to make sure I understand what each part says. The passage starts by talking about how even though April didn't bring rain, parents should be able to explain thunder and lightning to kids. Then it mentions that adults don't understand these phenomena well, which makes it hard to explain. The passage says that lightning we see flashing down from a cloud is actually flashing up from the earth to the cloud. Our eyes trick us into thinking it's downward. Then it gives an example about the sun rising and setting to emphasize that perception can be misleading. Next part: "Most lightning flashes take place inside a cloud, and only a few can be seen jumping between two clouds or between earth and a cloud." So most are inside clouds, and only a few are between clouds or between earth and cloud. That would mean that the majority are not between clouds or earth. So option B says "Lightning
